links.title: Main Service Debugging
links.tooltip: Open the main service debugging dashboard

panels.title: How to use this dashboard
panels.description: Dashboard notes and operating guidance.

options.content:
### Purpose
Focused debugging for `conorganizer-main.service` and the public main website.

Recommended workflow:
1. Confirm the service is active.
2. Check the current error signal.
3. Inspect error/panic/database logs.
4. Look at request rate, status codes, and latency.
5. Use slow/failing request logs to find concrete routes and request IDs.

Design choices:
- Service panels follow RED / Golden Signals: request rate, errors, duration/latency, and saturation proxies.
- HTTP behavior is log-derived from `msg="http request completed"`, `component="http"`, `method`, `path`, `status_code`, `duration_ms`, and optional `request_id`.
- No app Prometheus metric names are invented. Optional app-metric panels can be added later when `/metrics` exists.
- Loki queries start broad enough for `job=varlogs`; narrow `loki_job` or replace with exact journal labels after import.

Useful external examples:
- Loki Dashboard: https://grafana.com/grafana/dashboards/13186-loki-dashboard/
- Logging Dashboard via Loki v2: https://grafana.com/grafana/dashboards/18042-logging-dashboard-via-loki-v2/
- Prometheus Blackbox Exporter: https://grafana.com/grafana/dashboards/14928-prometheus-blackbox-exporter/

panels.title: Main service active
panels.description: Requires node_exporter systemd collector metrics. Alert candidate: conorganizer-main.service is not active.

panels.title: Main service active-state changes
panels.description: Proxy for restarts or service flapping. Requires node_exporter systemd collector metrics.

panels.title: Public main probe success
panels.description: Public blackbox probe for the main host. This is the symptom check when no app health endpoint exists.

panels.title: HTTP status: main
panels.description: Requires blackbox_exporter. Useful for spotting public failures even when app logs are incomplete.

panels.title: Recent main service logs
panels.description: Fallback selector for app/service logs. Replace with exact journal label selectors if available after import.

panels.title: Last startup or service failure logs
panels.description: Broad startup/runtime failure search for the main service.

targets.expr: {job=~"$loki_job"} |~ "conorganizer-main.service|conorganizer-main|error running server|could not initialize DB|event image directory|failed|panic|Main process exited"

panels.title: Current app error signal
panels.description: Error-like app log lines in the last 5 minutes. Green should be zero. Alert candidate: greater than 0.

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({job=~"$loki_job"} |~ "conorganizer-main|http request completed|database is locked|database is busy|panic|failed|error|ERROR|status_code" |~ "ERROR|error|failed|panic|status_code.:5[0-9][0-9]|database is locked|database is busy" [5m]))

panels.title: App error count
panels.description: Log-derived error trend. Includes 5xx request logs, panic, failed, ERROR, and database lock/busy signals.

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({job=~"$loki_job"} |~ "conorganizer-main|http request completed|database is locked|database is busy|panic|failed|error|ERROR|status_code" |~ "ERROR|error|failed|panic|status_code.:5[0-9][0-9]|database is locked|database is busy" [5m]))

panels.title: Server error logs
panels.description: App/service errors and 5xx request-completion logs. Use log details to inspect fields such as path, duration_ms, and request_id.

panels.title: Panic logs
panels.description: Any panic is operationally significant, even if recovered by middleware.

panels.title: Database lock/busy signal
panels.description: Focused SQLite contention signal in the last 5 minutes. Green should be zero.

panels.title: Recent database lock/busy logs
panels.description: Detailed SQLite lock/busy errors. Use this to correlate with slow requests and backup activity.

panels.title: Request rate from app logs
panels.description: Log-derived request rate from app request-completion logs.

panels.title: Status code rate
panels.description: Log-derived HTTP status-code rate. Requires JSON app request logs with status_code.

panels.title: 5xx count
panels.description: Log-derived 5xx count. Alert candidate: greater than 0.

panels.title: Top failing routes
panels.description: Groups 5xx request logs by path. Requires JSON request logs with path and status_code.

targets.expr: topk(10, sum by (path) (count_over_time({job=~"$loki_job"} |= "http request completed" | json | status_code >= 500 [1h])))

panels.title: 4xx count
panels.description: Client-error trend. Useful for spotting broken links, auth/session problems, or bots.

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({job=~"$loki_job"} |= "http request completed" | json | status_code >= 400 | status_code < 500 [5m]))

panels.title: Public root probe duration
panels.description: Blackbox probe duration for the public main host.

panels.title: p50 request latency
panels.description: Log-derived p50 latency from duration_ms.

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.50, {job=~"$loki_job"} |= "http request completed" | json | unwrap duration_ms | __error__="" [5m])

panels.title: p95 request latency
panels.description: Log-derived p95 latency from duration_ms. Tail latency is often the first useful saturation symptom.

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.95, {job=~"$loki_job"} |= "http request completed" | json | unwrap duration_ms | __error__="" [5m])

panels.title: p99 request latency
panels.description: Log-derived p99 latency from duration_ms. Investigate spikes with the slow request logs below.

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.99, {job=~"$loki_job"} |= "http request completed" | json | unwrap duration_ms | __error__="" [5m])

panels.title: Slow request logs > 1000 ms
panels.description: Request logs slower than 1 second. Inspect path, method, status_code, duration_ms, and request_id.

panels.title: Requests per minute
panels.description: Log-derived request volume.

panels.title: Requests by route
panels.description: Top request paths from JSON request logs.

targets.expr: topk(10, sum by (path) (count_over_time({job=~"$loki_job"} |= "http request completed" | json [5m])))

panels.title: Requests by method
panels.description: HTTP methods from JSON request logs.

panels.title: Top slow routes
panels.description: Maximum duration_ms by path in the last 15 minutes. Use with caution: a single outlier can dominate.

targets.expr: topk(10, max by (path) (max_over_time({job=~"$loki_job"} |= "http request completed" | json | unwrap duration_ms | __error__="" [15m])))

panels.title: Recent failed requests
panels.description: All recent 4xx/5xx request logs. Use path, status_code, duration_ms, and request_id to investigate.

panels.title: DB-related error count
panels.description: Log-derived DB symptom count. Includes app and migration-like messages.

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({job=~"$loki_job"} |~ "database is locked|database is busy|sqlite|SQLite|constraint failed|FOREIGN KEY|migration" [5m]))

panels.title: SQLite error logs
panels.description: SQLite and database errors from app logs.

targets.expr: {job=~"$loki_job"} |~ "database is locked|database is busy|sqlite|SQLite|constraint failed|FOREIGN KEY"

panels.title: Migration or startup DB logs
panels.description: DB startup/migration/integrity logs that can explain service boot failures.

panels.title: Optional SQLite DB size
panels.description: Optional future textfile/custom metric. Not expected until app or node textfile instrumentation is added.

panels.title: Optional SQLite WAL size
panels.description: Optional future textfile/custom metric. WAL growth can explain write pressure or backup/checkpoint issues.

panels.title: Mounted volume usage near app data
panels.description: Relevant saturation proxy for app database/images stored on the mounted volume.

panels.title: Recent app logs
panels.description: General app logs. Start here when the error panels show activity but the source is unclear.

panels.title: Recent app warnings and errors
panels.description: Warning/error-level app logs and error-like messages.

panels.title: Recent request logs
panels.description: Raw request-completion logs parsed as JSON. Use this to confirm path/status/duration fields are present.
